title: Capture is a branch, not an end
discovery: POW life needs its own state model because camp type, labor assignment, winter exposure, and legal status all change the lived experience after capture.
evidence: source_library/59_prisoner_of_war_camp.md:15-17 - detention, confinement, guard duty, and memory all matter after surrender.
source_library/60_german_prisoner_of_war_camps_world_war_ii.md:15-17 - camp type, labor, and nationality alter treatment.
source_library/64_stalag_ii_d.md:15-17 - a concrete camp example includes winter, nourishment, and civilian/prisoner conditions.
why: A surrender or capture event should branch the story instead of terminating it.

variable.name: captivity_stress
variable.definition: Stress caused by confinement, uncertainty, and deprivation after capture.

mechanic: Captivity mode with camp types, labor, and release paths.
pressure: Capture creates a separate life state that still generates decisions and memory.
